message: 🌱 Add kubeflow: Make arbitrary Jupyter images work with Kubeflow mission (#644)

files.patch: @@ -0,0 +1,76 @@ +{ + "version": "kc-mission-v1", + "name": "kubeflow-2208-make-arbitrary-jupyter-images-work-with-kubeflow", + "missionClass": "solution", + "author": "KubeStellar Bot", + "authorGithub": "kubestellar", + "mission": { + "title": "kubeflow: Make arbitrary Jupyter images work with Kubeflow", + "description": "Make arbitrary Jupyter images work with Kubeflow. Requested by 4+ users.", + "type": "feature", + "status": "completed", + "steps": [ + { + "title": "Check current kubeflow deployment", + "description": "Verify your kubeflow version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow\nhelm list -n kubeflow 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working kubeflow installation." + }, + { + "title": "Review kubeflow configuration", + "description": "Inspect the relevant kubeflow configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow\nkubectl get configmap -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubeflow\n```\nThere's a lot of good jupyter images already out there.\n\nFor example #767 discusses a Jupyter image with swift support for TensorFlow.\n\nWe'd also like to support images with R." + }, + { + "title": "Apply the fix for Make arbitrary Jupyter images work with Kubeflow", + "description": "Modified entrypoint command for jupyter images to launch jupyter on boot.\n\n---\nThis change is [](https://reviewable.io/reviews/kubeflow/kubeflow/2517)\n```yaml\ncontainer.Env = append(container.Env, corev1.EnvVar{\n\t\tName: \"NB_PREFIX\",\n\t\tValue: \"/notebook/\" + instance.Namespace + \"/\" + instance.Name,\n\t})\n```" + }, + { + "title": "Verify the feature works", + "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow\nkubectl get events -n kubeflow --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Make arbitrary Jupyter images work with Kubeflow\" is functioning correctly." + } + ], + "resolution": { + "summary": "Modified entrypoint command for jupyter images to launch jupyter on boot.\n\n---\nThis change is [](https://reviewable.io/reviews/kubeflow/kubeflow/2517)", + "codeSnippets": [ + "container.Env = append(container.Env, corev1.EnvVar{\n\t\tName: \"NB_PREFIX\",\n\t\tValue: \"/notebook/\" + instance.Namespace + \"/\" + instance.Name,\n\t})", + "# allow apache2 to use the NB_PREFIX provided by kubeflow\n# note: we strip off any trailing / for consistency\nshopt -s extglob;\necho \"export NB_PREFIX_NO_SLASH=${NB_PREFIX%%+(/)}\" >> /etc/apache2/envvars" + ] + } + }, + "metadata": { + "tags": [ + "kubeflow", + "incubating", + "app-definition", + "feature" + ], + "cncfProjects": [ + "kubeflow" + ], + "targetResourceKinds": [], + "difficulty": "intermediate", + "issueTypes": [ + "feature" + ], + "maturity": "incubating", + "sourceUrls": { + "issue": "https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/issues/2208", + "repo": "https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow", + "pr": "https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/pull/2517" + }, + "reactions": 4, + "comments": 16, + "synthesizedBy": "copilot" + }, + "prerequisites": { + "kubernetes": ">=1.24", + "tools": [ + "kubectl" + ], + "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible." + }, + "security": { + "scannedAt": "2026-03-11T06:25:41.909Z", + "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0", + "sanitized": true, + "findings": [] + } +}
